title: [cross-impl] schnorr48_verify_frame requires non-NULL payload even for len=0
description: C/Zephyr schnorr48_verify_frame (lichen/subsys/lichen/link/schnorr48.c:388) unconditionally rejects NULL payload pointer. Python (schnorr48.py:152) and Rust (schnorr.rs:187) accept empty msg (b"" or &[]). The simple schnorr48_verify allows msg=NULL when msg_len==0. This violates "same algorithm different behavior" for zero-length payloads in frame signing. Matches test/vectors/schnorr48.json vectors with empty messages. Last 20 commits (coap fixes + merges) did not address this core crypto consistency.
close_reason: Fixed cross-implementation inconsistency for empty payloads in schnorr48_verify_frame by updating NULL check to conditional on payload_len > 0. Updated header docs and nullability annotation. Tests pass. Commit 98f1e9d
